name: summitjs
description: The open source, AI Agent Native JavaScript framework. Add behavior directly in your HTML with signal-powered s- directives and $ magics. No build step, CSP-safe, about 16KB with focus, positioning, persistence, and masking built in.

directives.name: s-data
directives.summary: Declare reactive state on an element; descendants read and write it.

directives.name: s-text
directives.summary: Set the element's textContent from an expression.

directives.name: s-html
directives.summary: Set innerHTML from an expression (trusted content only).

directives.name: s-bind
directives.summary: Bind an attribute; :class and :style accept objects.

directives.name: s-on
directives.summary: Run an expression on a DOM event; supports modifiers.

directives.name: s-model
directives.summary: Two-way bind a form control to state.

directives.name: s-show
directives.summary: Toggle visibility with the display property.

directives.name: s-if
directives.summary: Add or remove from the DOM; a <template s-if> may hold multiple roots.

directives.name: s-for
directives.summary: Render a keyed list.

directives.name: s-ref
directives.summary: Name an element; read it via $refs.

directives.name: s-init
directives.summary: Run an expression once when the element initializes.

directives.name: s-effect
directives.summary: Re-run an expression whenever its reactive dependencies change.

directives.name: s-transition
directives.summary: Animate enter and leave.

directives.name: s-teleport
directives.summary: Render a template's content elsewhere in the DOM.

directives.name: s-intersect
directives.summary: Run an expression when the element enters or leaves the viewport.

directives.name: s-trap
directives.summary: Keep keyboard focus inside an element while an expression is truthy.

directives.name: s-anchor
directives.summary: Position an element next to a reference, flipping to stay in view.

directives.name: s-collapse
directives.summary: Animate an element open and closed by height.

directives.name: s-mask
directives.summary: Format an input as the user types against a pattern.

directives.name: s-cloak
directives.summary: Hide until initialized; pair with [s-cloak]{display:none}.

directives.name: s-ignore
directives.summary: Skip a subtree during initialization.

magics.name: $refs
magics.summary: Elements named with s-ref, by name.

magics.name: $root
magics.summary: The nearest enclosing s-data root element.

magics.name: $id
magics.summary: A stable unique id, scoped for pairing labels and controls.

magics.name: $store
magics.summary: Global reactive stores shared across components.

magics.name: $watch
magics.summary: Run a callback when a reactive expression changes.

magics.name: $nextTick
magics.summary: Run a callback after the next DOM update.

magics.name: $dispatch
magics.summary: Dispatch a custom DOM event that bubbles.

magics.name: $data
magics.summary: The reactive state object of the current scope.

magics.name: $persist
magics.summary: Reactive state backed by localStorage that survives reloads.

magics.name: $focus
magics.summary: Move keyboard focus around from an expression.

errors.summary: Unknown directive; the message suggests the closest real one.
A directive or event handler failed while evaluating its expression.
s-for must be used on a <template> element.
s-for <template> needs exactly one root element.
s-teleport must be used on a <template> element.
s-teleport target selector was not found in the DOM.
A cleanup callback threw during teardown.
A destroy() callback threw during teardown.

components.name: Button
components.summary: Buttons with solid, outline, ghost, subtle, and danger variants, plus sizes and groups.

components.name: Input
components.summary: Text inputs and textareas with labels, help text, addons, and validation state.

components.name: Select
components.summary: A styled native select bound with s-model.

components.name: Checkbox & Radio
components.summary: Accessible checkboxes and radio groups bound with s-model.

components.name: Switch
components.summary: A toggle switch bound with s-model, for on/off settings.

components.name: Card
components.summary: A surface for grouping content, with header, body, and footer.

components.name: Badge & Tag
components.summary: Small status labels and removable tags.

components.name: Alert
components.summary: Contextual feedback banners for info, success, warning, and danger.

components.name: Avatar
components.summary: User avatars with initials or images, sizes, and stacked groups.

components.name: Progress & Spinner
components.summary: Progress bars, spinners, and skeleton placeholders for loading states.

components.name: Tooltip
components.summary: A CSS-only tooltip that appears on hover and focus.

components.name: Dialog
components.summary: A modal dialog for confirmations and focused tasks, with overlay and Escape to close.

components.name: Dropdown Menu
components.summary: A button-triggered menu that closes on outside click or Escape.

components.name: Popover
components.summary: A click-triggered floating panel for rich content, dismissed on outside click or Escape.

components.name: Toast
components.summary: Transient notifications that stack and auto-dismiss.

components.name: Tabs
components.summary: Switch between panels with an underlined or soft tab bar.

components.name: Accordion
components.summary: Collapsible sections, single or multiple open.

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.summary: Show the path to the current page.

techniques.name: Loaders & Spinners
techniques.summary: A gallery of pure-CSS loaders: rings, dots, bars, shapes, animated logo marks, and skeleton screens.

techniques.name: Matte Glassmorphism
techniques.summary: Frosted, low-gloss glass surfaces that blur what is behind them while staying legible in both themes.

techniques.name: Neumorphism
techniques.summary: Soft, extruded UI built from twin light and dark shadows on a matching surface.

techniques.name: Claymorphism
techniques.summary: Puffy, rounded, playful clay surfaces with soft inner and outer shadows.

techniques.name: Retro UI
techniques.summary: Hard offset shadows and neon text for a retro, terminal, or arcade look.

techniques.name: Brutalism
techniques.summary: Stark borders, loud uppercase type, and blunt offset shadows for a raw, confident look.

techniques.name: Aurora Backgrounds
techniques.summary: Slow, glowing color fields that drift behind your content for a premium hero look.

techniques.name: Glow & Spotlight
techniques.summary: A soft highlight that follows the cursor across a card, driven by a tiny Summit handler.

techniques.name: Navbar Patterns
techniques.summary: Navigation bars with active links, a responsive mobile menu, and a glass variant, wired with Summit state.

techniques.name: Using Tailwind CSS
techniques.summary: Summit for behavior, Tailwind for styling. They compose cleanly because Summit lives in attributes and Tailwind lives in classes.
